title: cerastium alpinum subsp. glabratum
intro: Cerastium alpinum subsp. glabratum, also known as Mountain chickweed, is a sub-species of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Mountain chickweed include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and aid in the treatment of wounds and minor burns. Mountain chickweed can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used internally to address various health issues or applied topically to benefit the skin.
